tasks.title: Integrate Laws of the Night folder system into existing Laws Agent with file-based markdown loader
tasks.description: Extend the existing Laws Agent to support both database queries (rulebook_pages table) and file-based markdown indexing from agents/Laws_of_the_Night/ directory, creating a hybrid knowledge system that prioritizes file-based content while maintaining database fallback functionality.
tasks.details:
1. Create file-based markdown loader system:
   - Implement agents/laws_agent/markdown_loader.php class with recursive directory scanning
   - Index all .md files in agents/Laws_of_the_Night/ including subdirectories (chapters/, clans/, disciplines/)
   - Extract metadata from markdown frontmatter (title, category, tags, priority)
   - Build searchable index with full-text content and structured metadata
   - Implement caching mechanism using file modification timestamps for performance

2. Extend existing Laws Agent architecture:
   - Modify current database query logic to integrate with file-based system
   - Implement priority-based search that checks file index first, then database fallback
   - Create unified response format that combines results from both sources
   - Add source attribution (file path vs database record) to responses

3. Implement search and retrieval logic:
   - Use fuzzy matching algorithms (Levenshtein distance) for rule name queries
   - Implement semantic search using keyword extraction and relevance scoring
   - Support category-based filtering (chapters, clans, disciplines)
   - Create response ranking system that prioritizes exact matches and recent content

4. Database integration and migration:
   - Maintain backward compatibility with existing rulebook_pages queries
   - Add configuration option to enable/disable file-based vs database-only mode
   - Implement sync mechanism to update database with file-based content when needed
   - Create admin interface toggle for switching between search modes

5. Error handling and logging:
   - Implement comprehensive error handling for file system access issues
   - Add logging for indexing operations and search performance metrics
   - Create fallback mechanisms when file system is unavailable
   - Validate markdown parsing and handle malformed files gracefully
tasks.testStrategy: Create test markdown files in agents/Laws_of_the_Night/ with various structures (chapters, clans, disciplines) and verify successful indexing and retrieval. Test search functionality with exact rule names, partial matches, and category filters to ensure both file-based and database results are properly integrated. Verify performance by measuring response times for file indexing vs database queries with large datasets. Test error scenarios including missing directories, corrupted markdown files, and database connectivity issues to confirm graceful fallback behavior. Validate that existing Laws Agent functionality remains intact while new file-based features work correctly through A/B testing of responses.

**Analysis Results:**

Directory contains 84 markdown files across 3 subdirectories (chapters/, clans/, disciplines/) plus root-level TOC.md and metadata.json. File naming shows duplicates with numbered prefixes (e.g., Brujah.md vs 02_Clan_Brujah.md). YAML frontmatter includes title, chapter, section, and tags fields. Some files are content stubs referencing main chapters while others contain full content.

**Recommended Indexing Schema:**
- Prioritize numbered file versions over duplicates
- Extract and index YAML frontmatter metadata
- Full-text content indexing with category-based filtering
- File path tracking for citation purposes
- Metadata.json integration for structured navigation

subtasks.details:

Define search index schema and metadata extraction patterns.
<info added on 2025-11-15T01:28:53.023Z>
Architecture designed and implemented:

**Class: LawsOfTheNightLoader**
- Location: agents/laws_agent/markdown_loader.php
- Public API:
  - `loadIndex()` - Load and index all markdown files
  - `search($query, $category, $limit)` - Search indexed content
  - `getAllEntries()` - Get all indexed entries
  - `getStats()` - Get indexing statistics
  - `getExcerpt($content, $maxLength)` - Format content excerpts

**Data Structures:**
- Index array: Each entry contains id, file_path, relative_path, title, chapter, section, tags, category, content, content_lower, modified_time
- Category detection: chapter, clan, discipline, other
- Frontmatter extraction: title, chapter, section, tags (YAML parser)

**Features:**
- Recursive directory scanning
- YAML frontmatter parsing
- Category-based filtering
- Relevance scoring (title > section > content > tags)
- File modification time tracking
</info added on 2025-11-15T01:28:53.023Z>

tasks.title: Update Documentation with New Page Structure and Header/Footer Usage
tasks.description: Update the START_HERE.md file to document the new page structure, provide guidelines for header/footer usage, and update the version number to v0.5.0 throughout the application.
tasks.details:
1. Update the START_HERE.md file with the following sections:

   a. Application Structure:
   ```markdown
   ## Application Structure
   
   The Valley by Night application now uses a new page structure:
   - `index.php`: Main dashboard/landing page with role-based redirection
   - `player_dashboard.php`: Dashboard for regular players
   - `admin_dashboard.php`: Dashboard for admins and storytellers
   - `character_sheet.php`: Character viewing and editing (formerly index.php)
   - `includes/`: Directory containing reusable components
     - `header.php`: Standard header with Valley by Night styling
     - `footer.php`: Standard footer with Valley by Night styling
     - `character_queries.php`: Functions for character database operations
   ```
   
   b. Header and Footer Usage:
   ```markdown
   ## Using Header and Footer Components
   
   All pages should include the standardized header and footer components for consistent styling:
   
   ```php
   <?php
   // At the top of your file, after session initialization
   require_once 'includes/header.php';
   
   // Your page content here
   
   // At the bottom of your file
   require_once 'includes/footer.php';
   ?>
   ```
   
   The header component provides:
   - Valley by Night title and branding
   - User authentication display
   - Version number
   - Consistent gothic styling
   
   The footer component provides:
   - Copyright information
   - Return to home link
   - Consistent styling with the header
   ```
   
   c. Version Information:
   ```markdown
   ## Version Information
   
   Current version: v0.5.0
   
   This version includes:
   - Restructured application with separate dashboard views
   - Consistent header and footer components
   - Gothic styling throughout the application
   - Improved character management functions
   ```

2. Update the version number in relevant files:

   - Cream text: #f5e6d3

   - Created new index.php as main dashboard with role-based views

tasks.title: Create Bootstrap Migration Plan for VbN
tasks.description: Develop a comprehensive Bootstrap migration strategy that preserves current visuals pixel-for-pixel while creating detailed inventories, risk assessments, and phased implementation plans.
tasks.details: This task involves creating a complete Bootstrap migration plan without modifying any existing code. The deliverables include: (1) CSS/HTML Inventory - Conduct a thorough audit of all existing CSS files, HTML templates, and custom styles currently used in the VbN application. Document all selectors, properties, and their usage patterns. Create a comprehensive spreadsheet listing every custom CSS class, ID, and inline style with their locations and purposes. (2) Bootstrap Mapping & Risk Assessment - Map each identified custom CSS selector to equivalent Bootstrap utilities, components, or custom solutions. Assign risk levels (Low/Medium/High) based on complexity of migration, potential for visual breaking changes, and dependency on custom JavaScript. Document alternative approaches for high-risk items and identify areas requiring custom CSS extensions. (3) Phased Rollout Plan - Design a multi-phase implementation strategy starting with low-risk, isolated components and progressing to complex, interconnected elements. Define clear milestones, rollback procedures, and success criteria for each phase. Include timeline estimates and resource requirements. (4) Verification Framework - Establish pixel-perfect comparison methodologies using visual regression testing tools, cross-browser compatibility checks, and responsive design validation. Create approval checkpoints with stakeholder sign-off requirements before each implementation phase begins.
tasks.testStrategy: Validate the migration plan through: (1) Stakeholder review sessions to ensure all requirements are captured and the phased approach aligns with business priorities. (2) Technical feasibility assessment by conducting proof-of-concept migrations on 2-3 representative components to validate mapping accuracy and risk assessments. (3) Visual comparison testing using automated screenshot tools to establish baseline pixel-perfect standards. (4) Cross-functional team approval including development, design, and product management sign-offs on the complete plan before implementation begins. (5) Documentation review to ensure all deliverables are complete, accurate, and actionable for the development team.

tasks.title: Setup Project Structure and File Access
tasks.description: Initialize project structure and establish access to required reference files and directories
tasks.details: Create directory structure for Character Teasers if not exists. Verify access to missing-character-teasers.json, Valley_by_Night_Cinematic_Intro_Guide.md, and Valley_by_Night_Character_Art_Guide.json. Set up file paths and ensure write permissions to reference/Scenes/Character Teasers/ directory. Create tracking system for processed characters.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify all required files are accessible, directory structure is created, and test file write operations to target directory

tasks.title: Parse Character Data and Create Character Queue
tasks.description: Load and parse the missing-character-teasers.json file to create a processable queue of characters
tasks.details: Read missing-character-teasers.json and parse character entries. Extract key fields: id, name, notes, biography, appearance, clan, nature, demeanor, concept, equipmentTraits, negativeTraits, morality. Create data structure to track processing status. Implement character selection mechanism for iterative processing. Validate data integrity and handle missing fields gracefully.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify JSON parsing works correctly, all character fields are extracted properly, and queue management functions operate as expected

tasks.title: Implement Character Research System
tasks.description: Build system to gather comprehensive character information from VbN project files and relationships
tasks.details: Create research module that extracts character data from JSON entry and cross-references with VbN project files. Implement relationship mapping to identify connections with other characters. Build location database (Elysium, The Velvet Door, The Hole, havens, etc.) and character-location associations. Extract plot hooks and story connections from character notes. Analyze character's role in Phoenix Kindred society based on clan, nature, and background.
tasks.testStrategy: Test research system with sample characters, verify relationship mapping accuracy, and validate location associations

tasks.title: Create Concept Generation Engine
tasks.description: Develop system to generate three distinct concept summaries (Dark, Elegant, Mysterious) for each character
tasks.details: Build concept generator that creates exactly 3 two-sentence summaries per character: Dark (shadows, danger, moral ambiguity), Elegant (refinement, beauty, social grace), Mysterious (secrets, enigmatic presence). Implement location variability as primary differentiator between concepts. Integrate plot hook hints from character research. Ensure concepts align with character's clan, nature, and background. Include appropriate VbN locations and maintain neo-noir gothic tone.
tasks.testStrategy: Generate concepts for test characters, verify three distinct tones are achieved, and validate location variety and plot hook integration

tasks.title: Build User Interface for Concept Selection
tasks.description: Create interactive system for presenting concepts to user and capturing selection feedback
tasks.details: Implement user interface to display 3 concept options clearly formatted with Dark/Elegant/Mysterious labels. Provide selection mechanism (numbered choices or interactive menu). Include option for user to request modifications before proceeding. Implement feedback loop for concept refinement. Store user selection for use in full intro generation. Handle edge cases like invalid selections or modification requests.
tasks.testStrategy: Test UI with sample concepts, verify selection mechanism works correctly, and validate modification request handling

tasks.title: Implement Cinematic Intro Writer
tasks.description: Build system to generate complete 30-second cinematic intros following the Valley by Night format
tasks.details: Create intro writer that follows Valley_by_Night_Cinematic_Intro_Guide.md format exactly. Generate scene heading, cinematic description, character action/dialogue, and transitions. Target 30-second duration (7-9 shots, 5-7 lines). Include both formatted screenplay version and GM narration version. Implement signature fade-to-black + title card ending. Integrate selected concept, location, tone, and plot hooks. Apply neo-noir gothic styling with specified color palette (Deep Crimson #7A1E1E, Muted Gold #B89B64, Ash Gray #2F2F2F, Desert Amber #C87B3E). Include appropriate lighting and sound direction.
tasks.testStrategy: Generate test intros and verify format compliance, duration targeting, and style consistency with existing guide

tasks.title: Create File Output and Organization System
tasks.description: Implement system to save completed intros with proper naming and organization
tasks.details: Build file output system that saves completed intros to reference/Scenes/Character Teasers/[CharacterName].md using character's full name as filename. Implement proper markdown formatting for saved files. Ensure file naming follows existing conventions and handles special characters in names. Create backup mechanism and version control. Update tracking system to mark characters as completed. Implement file validation to ensure successful saves.
tasks.testStrategy: Test file saving with various character names, verify proper formatting and naming conventions, and validate file integrity

tasks.title: Implement Iterative Processing Workflow
tasks.description: Create main workflow system that processes characters one at a time with learning and feedback integration
tasks.details: Build main processing loop that handles one character at a time through the complete workflow for each character in missing-character-teasers.json: 1) Research character from JSON + VbN project (relationships, locations, plot hooks), 2) Analyze location/setting options (many at Elysium, some elsewhere), 3) Generate 3 two-sentence concept summaries (Dark, Elegant, Mysterious - location is most variable), 4) User selects concept, 5) Write full 30-second intro following Cinematic Intro Guide, 6) Save to reference/Scenes/Character Teasers/[CharacterName].md. Implement learning system that improves subsequent iterations based on user feedback patterns. Create progress tracking and resumption capability for interrupted sessions. Add workflow state management and error recovery. Implement feedback collection system to refine concept generation and intro quality. Include completion reporting and summary statistics.
tasks.testStrategy: Run complete workflow with test characters from missing-character-teasers.json, verify iterative processing works correctly through all 6 steps, validate file output to correct directory structure, and confirm learning improvements over multiple iterations
